original: King Otto II and his Frankish army defeated in pitched battle with Muslim army of al-Qasim at Cape Colonna, Southern Italy
Bayeux Tapestry likely first goes on display to decorate the nave during the consecration of Notre-Dame of Bayeux Cathedral in Bayeux, Normandy
Louis VIII becomes King of France upon the death of his father, Philip II
Battle at Vitkov Zizka's hill (Prague): Taboriets beat Bohemia
English Jesuit priest Edmund Campion arrested for sedition in Anglican England (later hung, drawn and quartered)
British composer Henry Purcell appointed organist of Chapel Royal, London
The Darien scheme begins with five ships, bearing about 1,200 people, departing Leith for the Isthmus of Panama
Battle of Aland, Russian fleet overpowers larger Swedish naval force
The de Portolá Expedition establishes a base in California and sets out to find the Port of Monterey (now Monterey, California)
Mission San Antonio de Padua founded in California
French National Convention decrees "La Marseillaise" by Claude-Joseph Rouget de Lisle as France's national anthem
First direct US federal tax on states enacted to finance military buildup; dwellings, land, and slaves are subject to tariff
US Sedition Act prohibits "any false, scandalous, and malicious writing" about the government
Date planned for the slave revolt in Charleston, South Carolina, by Denmark Vesey and Peter Poyas; plot already uncovered in June
Switzerland signs boundaries for fugitives
First postmasters' provisional stamps issued in New York City
First public demonstration of ice made via refrigeration by Florida physician John Gorrie
Commodore Perry requests trade relations with Japan
New Zealand holds its first general election
General McDowell advances toward Fairfax Courthouse, Virginia with 40,000 troops
Naval Engagement at Wilmington North Carolina - USS Daylight establishes blockade
Battle of Falling Waters, Maryland (Beaver Creek)
Jews of Holstein Germany granted equality
Gold is discovered in Helena, Montana
English mountaineer Edward Whymper leads the first expedition to climb the Matterhorn; four die on the descent
The Chickasaw Nation is the last of the Five Tribes to surrender to the Union following the Confederate defeat
Alvin J. Fellows of New Haven, Connecticut, patents the tape measure
British battleship HMS Thunderer boiler explodes during full-power sea trial near Portsmouth, England; 45 sailors killed and 40 injured
General strike brings US railroad to a standstill
The Temple Street Cable Railway begins cable car service in Los Angeles, California
American John T. Smith patents corkboard
The Campanile in St Mark's Square, Venice collapses, also demolishing the loggetta (reconstructed 1912)
46 inches of rain begins to fall in Baguio, Philippines
Kenneth McArthur runs an Olympic record marathon in 2:36:54.8
Rolex receives the first wristwatch Class 'A' certificate from Kew Observatory
13.2 inches (33.6 cm) of rainfall at Effingham, South Carolina (state record until 1999)
St Louis Brown Ernie Koob pitches all 17 innings for 14 hits in a 0-0 tie vs. Boston Red Sox at Fenway Park
Nicola Sacco and Bartolomeo Vanzetti convicted of killing their shoe company's paymaster and sentenced to death, in Dedham Massachusetts
First commercial airplane flight in Hawaii
Belgian Chamber rules Dutch language for education in Flanders
All non-Nazi political parties are banned in Germany
Germany begins mandatory sterilization of people with hereditary illnesses
Hedley Verity bowls out Essex twice in a day: 8-47 and 9-44 for Yorkshire at Leyton
116°F (47°C) is recorded in Orogrande, New Mexico, setting a state record, which is later broken on June 27, 1994
Philadelphia Phillies score 11 runs in an inning, beating Cincinnati 18-0
116°F (47°C) recorded in Collegeville, Indiana (state record)
One million people demonstrate to support French People's Front government
Due to beanball wars, Spalding advertises batting helmets with earflaps
Lithuania becomes Lithuanian SSR
6,000 Lithuanian Jews are exterminated at Viszalsyan Camp by German Einsatzgruppen SS with the assistance of local collaborators
Ceasefire of Joan of Arc, which ends combat in Lebanon and Syria
Jam rationed in Holland
First transport of Amsterdam Jews to Westerbork
Riots against Jews in Amsterdam
Attempt to liberate prisoners in Amsterdam fails; John Post arrested
US assault on Coutances Cotentin
Battleship USS South Dakota is first US ship to bombard Japan
Cleveland's Lou Boudreau hits four doubles and a home run, but the Red Sox win 11-10 onTed Williams' three home runs with eight RBIs
Kielce pogrom: A mob of civilians, police, and soldiers investigating a false kidnapping allegation attacks Jewish Holocaust survivors in Kielce, Poland; 42 are killed, and more than 40 are injured
R.E. Wayne is awarded the first Distinguished Flying Cross in Korea
First color telecast of a sporting event (CBS - horse race)
In his last race, 1948 Triple Crown champion Citation wins the Hollywood Gold Cup by four lengths to become American racing's first millionaire horse
American ocean liner SS United States crosses Atlantic Ocean, returning to New York in a westward record time of 84 hours and 12 minutes
117°F (47°C) recorded in East St. Louis, Illinois (state record)
118°F (48°C) recorded in Warsaw and Union, Missouri (state record)
Two killed and many dazed by lightning strikes at Ascot racecourse in England
Boston Red Sox pitcher Mel Parnell no-hits Chicago White Sox, 4-0 at Fenway Park, Boston
Soviet steamer "Eshghbad" sinks in Caspian Sea, drowning 270
Iraqi Army brigadier Abd al-Karim Qasim forms a military government after overthrowing the Iraqi monarchy in the 14 July Revolution
USS Long Beach, first nuclear powered cruiser launched at Quincy, Massachusetts
Fire rages through a Guatemala City insane asylum killing 225 and severely injuring 300
Finland's Miettunen government forms
Borehole for Mont Blanc tunnel finished
US performs nuclear Test at Nevada Test Site
MLB Baltimore Oriole Bob Johnson's 6th straight hit as a pinch hitter
Australian Ron Clarke runs world record in the 10,000 meters (27:39.4)
Israeli Jordanian border fights
US Mariner IV, the first Mars probe, passes at 6,100 miles (9,800 km)
Richard Speck rapes and kills eight nurses in a Chicago dormitory
Houston Astro Eddie Mathews hits his 500th home run off SF GiantJuan Marichal
Surveyor 4 launched to Moon and explodes just before landing
Hank Aaronhits his 500th home run off San Francisco Giant Mike McCormick
Houston Astro Don Wilson strikes out 18, beats Reds 6-1 at Crosley Field in Cincinnati
WSWO TV channel 26 in Springfield, OH (ABC) begins broadcasting
A 67-year-old Catholic civilian dies after being attacked by RUC officers in Dungiven; many consider this the first death of "The Troubles"
The United States $500, $1,000, $5,000, and $10,000 bills are officially withdrawn from circulation
WMUL (now WPBY) TV channel 33 in Huntington, WV (PBS) broadcasts for first time
Jean Westwood is first woman to head Democratic National Committee
Plate umpire and catcher in a game are brothers; Bill Haller is umpire and Tom Haller is Detroit Tigers catcher; Kansas City Royals win, 1-0
USSR performs underground nuclear test

Plans are announced for EPCOT Center at Disney World in Orlando, Florida
USSR banishes dissident Andrei Amalrik to Netherlands
North Korea shoots down US helicopter, killing three
US House establishes permanent Select Committee on Intelligence
Anatoly Scharansky convicted of anti-Soviet agitation
USSR performs nuclear test
Kevin Wade's "Key Exchange" premieres in London
Mario Bros. is first released by Nintendo in Japan as an arcade game featuring an Italian-American plumber
US politicians Dan Crane (Rep-R-Il) and Gerry Studds (Rep-D-Mas) admit to sex with pages
STS 41-D vehicle moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for remanifest of payloads
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Columbia returns to Kennedy Space Center via Offutt AFB, Neb
Last USFL game-Baltimore Stars defeats Oakland Invaders, 28-24
An ETA bomb attack in Madrid kills 10 and injures 60
Motley Crue's Vince Neil begins 30-day sentence for vehicular homicide
NASA's plan to implement recommendations of Rogers Commission
Richard Miller becomes the first FBI agent convicted of espionage
Second Lubbers government formed in the Netherlands
Shalamar's Howard Hewett acquitted of drug charges in Miami
Greyhound Bus buys Trailways Bus for $80 million
Taiwan ends 37 years of martial law
200,000 demonstrate in Soviet Armenia for incorporation of Nagorno-Karabak

Sara Martin of Illinois is crowned America's Junior Miss
Failed military coup in Mali
Actress Nell Carter undergoes brain surgery
Aeroflot starts non-stop flights between Moscow and NY
Gas explosion at an old age home in Milan kills 27
LA Dodger Ramón Martinez no-hits the Florida Marlins 7-0
New York Yankees closer John Wetteland sets MLB record 24 consecutive saves
NY Yankees sweep complete season series in Baltimore for first time
Bomb in Algiers kills 21 and wounds 40
Violence erupts in Richmond, South Africa, reflecting underlying political tensions between supporters of the ANC and Inkatha Freedom Party

Singapore experiences a seasonally adjusted and annualised growth of 20.4% in the second quarter as a result of increased pharmaceuticals production and construction, bringing the country out of the recession
Floods on the Japanese island of Kyushu kill 20 and displace 250,000
Suicide bomber attacks a wedding reception and kills 22 people and injures 22 in northern Afghanistan
After 7 days, Israel has struck Gaza 1320 times, while Gaza has fired 940 rockets at Israel
The Church of England votes in favor of allowing women to become bishops
The death toll from the West African Ebola outbreak passes 500
The Egyptian government propose a cease fire in the Israel–Gaza conflict
Arms deal agreed between 6 world powers and Iran limiting Iranian nuclear arms but ending sanctions
Scientists from the Large Hadron Collider announce the discovery of a new particle called the pentaquark
Civilians killed in Afghan war reach record high with 1,692 killed in the first six months of 2018, says UN
Haitian Prime Minister Jack Guy Lafontant resigns after violent protests against fuel price rises
Huge iceberg looming over Innaarsuit village, western Greenland prompts evacations in case it calves
Russian covert agent Maria Butina arrested by the FBI in Washington, D.C. and charged with being an unregistered foreign agent
First Ebola case on Goma, Eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, city of one million, as toll of outbreak reaches 1600 victims
New study shows Andean condor, world's heaviest bird, can fly for 5 hours without flapping its wings (Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences)
Drug overdose deaths in the US rose by 30% to a record 93,000 in 2020, according to the CDC
India launches its Chandrayaan-3 spacecraft from the Sriharikota Space Centre on the country's third moon mission
US architect Rex Heuermann charged with 3 out of 11 possible murders in the Gilgo Beach murders dating back to 2007 on Long Island, New York
Evian Championship Women's Golf, Evian Resort GC: Japan's Ayaka Furue eagles the final hole to defeat Stephanie Kyriacou of Australia by 1 stroke for her first major title
American billionaire Alice Walton opens the Alice L. Walton School of Medicine in Arkansas, United States, and covers tuition for the first five graduating classes
French woman Gisèle Pelicot receives the Legion of Honour award for her courage in testifying about her decades-long sexual abuse by her husband
EmperorCharles Vconquers Tunis
PopePius Vintroduces a standardised Roman Missal (text of the Latin mass), a reform of the Council of Trent. Will remain unchanged for 400 years.
The Fête de la Fédération, attended byLouis XVIandMarie Antoinette, is held across France on the first anniversary of the French Revolution
The Priestley Riots driveJoseph Priestley, a supporter of the French Revolution, out of Birmingham, England
US PresidentFranklin Pierceopens the "Exhibition of the Industry of All Nations" (a World's Fair) at the Crystal Palace in New York City (now site of Bryant Park)

German ChancellorBernhard von Bülowresigns and is replaced byTheobald von Bethmann Hollweg

18th ESPY Awards:Drew Brees,Lindsey Vonnwin
